subject: Add demo, heatbridge,eteutils back
commit_message:
Add demo, heatbridge,eteutils back

interaction with jjb was missed when change to COPY . was made

Issue-ID: TEST-229
Change-Id: Ida0289d8d1bcbaa9759b00b09ae552e0575c2efb
Signed-off-by: Brian Freeman <bf1936@att.com>

entries.message: Uploaded patch set 1.
Uploaded patch set 2.

Patch Set 2:

(2 comments)

See comments on two files that get over written by testsuite/oom charts
Patch Set 2: Code-Review+2

Patch Set 2: Code-Review-1

(1 comment)

as mentioned in the mail,
I am not very fond of Dockerfile depending on pre-processing from Jenkins
Jenkins or gitlab-ci should clone the repo then launch the build, nothing more.

When you read such dockerfile it is confusing because you do not know where demo, heatbridge, python-utils are coming from, so your dockerfile cannot be simply used (if you clone the repo, run a docker buid ./docker it will fail).

I would prefer a solution as I did on Daniel's patch (I should have done it on master)
See the pip install git+https + git clone inside the Docker, I think it is a good way to include third party repositories and keep them under control. 
As everything is done in the docker, there is no dependencies on CI system. The Docker becomes self-sufficient.
I also added a requirements.txt to collect the python requirements

But this patch as my patchest #6 in 97660 is very different from the patch initiated by Daniel...
it fixed the missing dir but it remains confusing

------------------------------------------------------------------------------

FROM ubuntu
## Be careful of Windows newlines
MAINTAINER "ONAP"
LABEL name="Docker image for the ONAP Robot Testing Framework"
LABEL usage="docker run -e ROBOT_TEST=<testname> -ti onapete"
ENV BUILDTIME=true
	
ARG ONAP_TAG=master
	
COPY requirements.txt requirements.txt
	
# Install Python, Pip, Robot framework, chromium, lighttpd web server
RUN apt-get update \
    && apt-get install \
        --no-install-recommends \
        --assume-yes \
            chromium-browser \
            chromium-chromedriver \
            dnsutils \
            git \
            gcc \
            libffi-dev \
            libssl-dev \
            lighttpd \
            make \
            net-tools \
            python2.7 python-dev python-setuptools python-wheel python-pip \	
            netbase \
            unzip zip \
            x11-utils x11-xserver-utils \
            xvfb \
            xxd  \
            wget vim  \
            python3.7 python3.7-dev python3-pip && \
    pip install -r requirements.txt && \
    pip install --no-cache-dir \
    git+https://git.onap.org/testsuite/heatbridge.git@$ONAP_TAG#egg=heatbridge\&subdirectory=heatbridge  \
    git+https://git.onap.org/testsuite/python-testing-utils.git@$ONAP_TAG#egg=robotframework-onap\&subdirectory=robotframework-onap && \
    git clone --depth 1 https://git.onap.org/testsuite -b $ONAP_TAG /var/opt/ONAP && \
    git clone --depth 1 https://git.onap.org/demo -b $ONAP_TAG /src/demo
	
RUN python3.7 -m pip install setuptools wheel
RUN python3.7 -m pip install virtualenv
# Copy the robot code
RUN mkdir -p /etc/lighttpd && \
    rm /etc/lighttpd/lighttpd.conf && \
    ln -s /var/opt/ONAP/docker/lighttpd.conf /etc/lighttpd/lighttpd.conf && \
    ln -s /var/opt/ONAP/docker/authorization /etc/lighttpd/authorization && \
    chmod 777 /var/opt/ONAP/setup.sh \
	
    && chmod 777 /var/opt/ONAP/runTags.sh \
    && chmod 777 /var/opt/ONAP/dnstraffic.sh \
    && chmod 777 /var/opt/ONAP/runSoak.sh \
    && chmod 777 /var/opt/ONAP/runEteTag.sh \
    && chmod 600 /var/opt/ONAP/robot/assets/keys/* && \
    cd /var/opt/ONAP && ./setup.sh \
	
	
    && apt-get autoremove --assume-yes \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*  \
    && apt-get clean
CMD ["lighttpd", "-D", "-f",  "/etc/lighttpd/lighttpd.conf"]

Patch Set 2: Code-Review-1

(1 comment)

I +1 Morgan statement (and so -1 this review)

Patch Set 2:

And fyi, as Morgan already said, 
testsuite/oom will be reintegrated into OOM main repo just after Frankfurt version is out (or sooner) as it's a nightmare to coordinate those two repos and lead to Healtchcheck failures that are lasting weeks (CLI, NBI, ...) and I don't want that anymore

Abandoned

OK - quick fix till full plan is created doesn't seem to be needed.

Patch Set 2:

So will aai oom project repo be moved back into oom as well ? the whole idea was to empower the projects to do their own helm charts as consistent updates so a project config change and matching helm charts could be done as one thing by the project - seems like moving the health checks into the project repo would be a better way to match health check changes with the project changes ?

Patch Set 2:

> Patch Set 2:
> 
> So will aai oom project repo be moved back into oom as well ? the whole idea was to empower the projects to do their own helm charts as consistent updates so a project config change and matching helm charts could be done as one thing by the project - seems like moving the health checks into the project repo would be a better way to match health check changes with the project changes ?

It is like git submodules, it sounds like a good idea but it is not really at the end. 😊
when there is a change in AAI impacting a test, we need to sync 3 repos, which is not possible
moreover the skills in helm charts are limited, if you allow all the projects to do their charts, it become unmanageable for the OOM team (hardcoded passwords, templating, security, linting, divergence, lots of DBs/versions versus unification effort, docker optimization,..)

You are right, projects would probably feel more committed to the healthcheck tests if they had to host them in their repository. I think integration could suggest that for Guiling. Another option is to keep the robot part in testsuite but get new healthcheck from the projects (like policy and nbi)

Patch Set 2:

> Patch Set 2:
> 
> So will aai oom project repo be moved back into oom as well ? the whole idea was to empower the projects to do their own helm charts as consistent updates so a project config change and matching helm charts could be done as one thing by the project - seems like moving the health checks into the project repo would be a better way to match health check changes with the project changes ?


That's my intention yes.

When you look on how a simple Dockerfile is done by some projects (double processes, "I install curl and nc cause I like to debug even in production", ...), I'm pretty sure that no project is able to handle correctly its own helm charts.

They still can propose changes in OOM but Krzysztof (O. not K. ;) ) and I will be able to take a look and verify that all the charts are (becoming) consistents.

We've got a long way before the project becomes cloud natives and their charts are super simple. Look at SO for example, all the projects are in the same git repo, meaning it's impossible to update one without building the others. This is not "cloud native".

And we have countless examples on ONAP these days.
When we'll have strong components, reliable deployments, it will be time to see how we can give more freedom for the project.

But I'm sure that today it will be a mistake (and AAI breaking once in a while -- although a lot less now they wait for us to say +1 -- is the perfect proof for me)

Patch Set 2:

So good discussion.

I have seen oom helm charts break projects since the PTL is not always included in chart changes that cause their projects to break so probably want to tighten that process up a bit.

That being said - robot is broken - distribution and instantiation is blocked for Guilan.

When will a fix be in place so that we have a docker container that works for healthdist, isntantiateVWCL , instantiateVFWDT, instantiatevCPEInfra , etc ?

today healthdist is successuly run on OOM Master (pre-frankfurt) everyday and on every gating so we can do some automatic testing but we need a robot pod in good shape to use the other scripts especially the the 3 ones you are mentioning.

I would like to run isntantiateVWCL, instantiateVFWDT, instantiatevCPEInfra the same way (as pnf_registrate)
But I think these use cases are dependending on the installation (need networks and preconditions done through the onap_overide.yaml) and cannot be run as a simple robot tag. Do you confirm that demo init is needed prior to the 3 instantiation tests.

I think the patch I submitted should fix the 3rd party repo existing issue.
Kryzstof K. made recent tests and was able to run demo init.

I do not clearly see how far we are if we move to Daniel new docker (as far as I understand in his mail, it is already used in ATT, so we shall not be very far)
I hope I could give a try tomorrow.
We should decide quickly if it is achievable for Frankfurt..or not.

Regarding the problem of instantiation, I am not sure it is 100% due to the robot at the moment - we got also an issue when performing E2E tests (basic_vm) with onap-test framework (working fine in El Alto). I suspect some changes in A&AI/SDNC (as we had in SDC with the new onboarding procedure). we got a SDNC/AAI issue on module creation.

Since healthdist requires the demo repo to be in the container I dont understand how it could be working. Is the gating using the latest robot docker ?

> 
> demo init is required before instantiate but not for healthdist.
> Since healthdist requires the demo repo to be in the container I dont understand how it could be working. Is the gating using the latest robot docker ?

healthdist (as well as healthcheck) is working because it is run from a xtesting docker - not from the robot pods -

it is run as a job in the cluster, and the docker image used is the one built here https://gitlab.com/Orange-OpenSource/lfn/onap/integration/xtesting/container_registry
this docker includes well the demo repository as well as the other third party repositories
it is rebuilt everyday so it takes into account all the new changes done in all the repositories.

but gitlab.com is very ... practical...built-in registry, security dashboard, free (self-service) CI
rewriting stuff for jenkins assuming that it already works and does the job is not easy..
